title.rendered: An Inspiring Evening with David Suzuki & Tara Cullis!

content.rendered:
David Suzuki and Tara Cullis are coming to the Burlington Performing Arts Centre (BPAC) on March 8th, and BurlingtonGreen is proud to be a partner for this special event.

What You Won’t Do For Love is a special event that is expected to sell out.

Join us for a powerful opportunity to gather, reflect, and feel inspired by voices that have shaped environmental action for decades.

SPECIAL OFFER: Sign up to receive BurlingtonGreen’s popular e-newsletter, and you’ll receive a promo code for 25% off your tickets. (while tickets remain available)

You can also purchase tickets here, while seats remain.

EVENT TICKETS


What others are saying:

“A deeply personal, totally enchanting love story. David and Tara take us on a magical journey into the heart of commitment – to one another, to family, to community, to justice and to the planet. A testament to hope.”

– Maude Barlow (Author and activist, founding member of the Council of Canadians, co-founder of the Blue Planet Project)

“Warm, wise and overflowing with generosity, this is a love story so epic it embraces all of creation. Yet another reminder of how blessed we are to be in the struggle with elders like David and Tara.”

– Naomi Klein (author, journalist, social activist, and filmmaker) and Avi Lewis (filmmaker, climate activist, educator)

What You Won’t Do For Love is a special event that is expected to sell out. Join us for a powerful opportunity to gather, reflect, and feel inspired by voices […]

title.rendered: Decoding Bird Names with Bob Bell

content.rendered:
Date:Thursday, March 12th, 2026
Time: 7pm
Location: Zoom Webinar

Register for this FREE event further below.

Join us for an interactive and educational birding webinar with Bob Bell, aka The Birder Bob, on Thursday, March 12th, from 7:00–8:15 pm on Zoom.

Have you ever wondered why some bird names are so confusing? Bob will demystify them in a fun and approachable way. You’ll also get a chance to test your bird ID skills with photos, songs, and calls.

Book Giveaway!

Thanks to Bob’s generosity, one webinar participant will win a copy of his book Out of the Lyme Light and Into the Sunlight: Birding as Therapy for the Chronically Ill.

A great evening for curious beginners and seasoned birders alike.

Bob has previously given six talks for BurlingtonGreen, which are available for free to view on our YouTube channel:

Backyard Birding with Bob Bell

Out of the Lyme Light: Wellness Through Birding with Bob Bell

Introduction to Birding in Burlington and Hamilton with Bob Bell

The Mystique of Owls: An Introduction to Owling in Ontario with Bob Bell

The Wonderful World of Warblers with Bob Bell

Mysteries of Migration Birding Webinar with Bob Bell

Bob would be delighted to have you follow his book and birding activity, by sending a friend request on Facebook, or by following him on Bluesky and Instagram.

Bob’s book, “Out of the Lyme Light and Into the Sunlight: Birding as Therapy for the Chronically Ill” can be bought locally at Wild Birds Unlimited and A Different Drummer Books in Burlington, and at King W. Books in Hamilton. It is also available online from Amazon, Indigo, or directly from the publisher.

More about Bob Bell:

Bob Bell spent 35 years as a mineral exploration geologist before retiring to Ancaster in 2015. He quickly discovered that the area, with its multitude of habitats and environments, was fantastic for birding.

Bob is passionate about sharing the joy of birding with people of all skill levels and enjoys introducing them to the huge variety of beautiful birds that can be seen in the Hamilton and Burlington areas.

He hopes that his passion will inspire others to take in the sights and sounds of nature that surround us, and that this interest will translate into support and protection of local birds.

PLEASE COMPLETE AND SUBMIT THE FORM TO REGISTER FOR THIS EVENT:

Learn more about BurlingtonGreen’s Nature Friendly Burlington Program.

Sign up for our monthly Eco Newsletter to learn about more events, opportunities and eco-living tips!

GIVE TODAY FOR A GREENER TOMORROW

content.rendered:
Join Reconnect Burlington and BurlingtonGreen to swap, connect, and make an impact at Burlington’s Fall Clothing & Toy Swap!

🗓 Saturday, November 8, 2025
🕙 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM
📍 St. Christopher’s Church, Burlington (map)

Bring your gently used items to exchange for new-to-you treasures — all while helping reduce waste and finding some gifts to help spread holiday joy.

REGISTER TO ATTEND

Why come?

Enjoy a meaningful day with:

👕 A Clothing & Toy Swap
💬 Participate in community games
📖 Learn about the impact of fashion
🎨 Colouring Activity for Kids
🧵 Mending Station for simple fixes
☕ Free refreshments for all participants (while supplies last)

What to bring

Clean clothing, shoes, hats, accessories, and toys in good working condition — items you’d feel comfortable gifting to someone else.

Your family, and friends – the more, the merrier! Registrants will receive an extra raffle ticket for each extra person you bring with you.

How the swap works:

10:00–11:00 AM: Items drop-off
11:00 AM – 11:30 AM: Brief presentation & Swap instructions
11:30 AM–1:00 PM: Swap time

Come for the swap, stay for the community!

Whether you’re clearing out your closets, refreshing your wardrobe, or looking for holiday gift ideas with heart—this event is for you!

♻️ Any leftover items will be donated to the Compassion Society. Please remember to bring only clean items that are in good working condition.

PS: Children must be supervised by a parent or guardian at all times.

Reconnect Burlington & BurlingtonGreen are working together to keep usable items out of landfills, reduce waste, and strengthen our community.

Learn more about the Reconnection Project Here

content.rendered:
Saturday, October 18, 2025 from 10 am to 4 pm. Indoor activities: 10:30 am to 3:30 pm.

Held in partnership with the Burlington Centre  and Tech Genius Burlington Inc, we’re excited to host another impactful event at the Burlington Centre’s convenient parking area (corner of Guelph Line & Fairview) to accept computer and cell phone electronics.

Discover below how you can further grow the impact of these events by organizing collections with your networks!

There’s more. A series of Terracycle Zero Waste  bins are also on site to accept more unique waste items not currently accepted in Halton Region’s recycling program. And you can even drop off wooden chopsticks to the ChopValue bin we will have available at the event.

Pesky candy wrappers, Brita water filters, reusable plastic containers and lids and used PPE can be dropped off, along with batteries and ink cartridges.

Battery Collection & check out a fire truck! – the Burlington Fire Department will be on hand to collect used batteries, share lithium-ion safety tips, and swap out smoke alarms over 10 years old (while supplies last). Check out a real fire truck from 2:00 – 3:00 p.m. — unless duty calls!

NEW this year: Reducing Plastic Pollution

After dropping off your items outside, come inside between 10:30 AM and 3:30 PM to explore our interactive space on plastic pollution. Families can enjoy games and hands-on activities for all ages, while adults and youth can dive deeper into solutions — from simple everyday swaps to letter writing tips and petitions that call on companies and elected officials to take action on plastic waste.

With resources from Environmental Defence’s Plastic Pollution Campaign, this space offers something for everyone — fun, learning, and ways to use your voice for change.

This portion of the Zero Waste drop-off is brought to you in partnership with Reconnect Burlington.

Further details about this popular event in addition to a list of what can be dropped off at the Zero-Waste Drop-Off event is available here.

LEARN MORE HERE!

WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 24TH @ 7 PM ONLINE WEBINAR!

The always popular Kyle McLoughlin, Master Arborist and Supervisor of Forest Planning and Health at the City of Burlington, joins us as our local subject matter expert.

Kyle will share another fascinating presentation with time to follow to address tree-related questions.

We invite you to send us your tree-related questions when you register or email them to us before September 24th.

*Please note: If you have specific questions about Burlington’s tree-bylaw, city tree planting/removing/trimming, please visit the City of Burlington website.

BONUS! All Burlington resident attendees will be entered into a draw for a chance to win a FREE tree!

Learn about more fall eco-opportunities here.

Confirm your participation by completing and submitting the following form:

Unpublished form

Click here to learn about more of our fall events and opportunities!

Voting is NOW CLOSED for the 2025 Burlington Tree Photo contest.

The photo receiving the most community votes was PHOTO # 3!

The winner will receive a $50 gift card to Connon Nurseries.

A big thank you to everyone who kindly submitted a photo this year, and to everyone who took the time to vote.

We can all look forward to next year’s Burlington Tree Photo contest, while continuing to enjoy all these beautiful photos.

content.rendered:
Let’s learn and care for trees, together!

Open to local residents, students, groups, business employees, while space remains, this popular tree-ific event is fun, rewarding, and educational too!

Hosted by BurlingtonGreen and City of Burlington Forestry Staff, this year’s TLC (Tree Loving Care) event will bring community members together to carry out some hands-on stewardship activities at a former tree planting location at Pathfinder Park, in Burlington. The event will take place on Saturday, September 27th from 9 am to 11:30 am. Space is limited for this opportunity. Advance registration is required – below.

Together we will plant some trees, do a tree inventory, care for previously planted trees by doing some weeding and adding mulch, and we will create and scatter some native seed balls too!

BONUS: participants will have a chance to win a beautiful native tree to take home to plant in Burlington!

The TLC event requires advance registration and will take place rain or shine.

This project is also supported by the Reconnect Project. Click here to learn more!

REGISTER FOR THIS EVENT BY COMPLETING AND SUBMITTING THE FOLLOWING FORM:

Unpublished form

Learn about more fall eco-opportunities here.

Thank you to the following event partners/supporters:

content.rendered:
You can express your gratitude this Thanksgiving when you join together with other community members to clean up Burlington Beach.

Come on your own, or gather family and friends.

Let’s clean up the beach together.

REGISTER TODAY BY COMPLETING AND SUBMITTING THE FORM FURTHER BELOW

There will also be an opportunity to add your personal note to our Thanksgiving Gratitude Tree and add your Eco Actions to help us reach our goal of 200,000 eco actions in Burlington by 2030!

What to Bring:

– Weather-appropriate clothing and sturdy footwear (waterproof boots can be handy for reaching litter in the water)
– Your reusable water bottle
– Reusable gloves (We’ll have plenty to borrow)
– Litter picker tool if you have one (not required, we have a limited supply)
– Lots of energy and enthusiasm!

This FREE event is open to all Burlington community members who care about the environment. Accessible options are available for those with mobility limitations.

Let’s come together to express our gratitude and take action to protect our local natural spaces, making a difference, one piece of litter at a time.

See you at the beach!

This free event is part of our Thanksgiving Weekend at the Beach programming, which will include eco-action and learning opportunities October 11th to 13th. Look for more details on our social media and in our October EcoNews (sign up to receive our EcoNews here).

Unable to read published form data

Learn about more fall eco-opportunities here.

title.rendered: BG Fun for Kids at RBG!

content.rendered:
Take a nature walk, make a seedball and join other eco-fun with BG at Royal Botanical Gardens on October 4th and 5th.

BurlingtonGreen will have a tent at the annual Telling Tales Children’s Literary Festival in Hendrie Park at the RBG.

Here’s our schedule of activities:

Daily 9:45 am – 5 pm – Drop by the BG tent to make your own native wildflower seed balls and learn how you can help protect and nurture butterflies and other pollinators. We’ll also have other all-ages eco-fun and climate action resources.

Daily at 11:00 am, 2:00 pm and 3:30 pm – Join a Nature Discovery Walk with BG. During this 30 minute guided walk, you’ll discover fascinating facts about nature all around us. Get inspired to be an eco-champion for special friends like amazing Monarch Butterflies, and afterwards you can stay to make seedballs to grow wildflowers that can attract butterflies and other pollinators to your own backyard or balcony.

Free entry with registration here.
Then Come find the BG tent in Hendrie Park at the RBG!!

Telling Tales is a weekend of authors, storytelling, creativity and fun! 📖📚🌎

Learn about more fall eco-opportunities

content.rendered:
Located at the historic Pump House at 1094 Lakeshore Rd, Beachway Park, pop by our Eco-Hub for a variety of eco-awareness, advocacy, and action opportunities.

10 am – 4 pm
Weekends and holidays (late April to Labour Day weekend), Thanksgiving weekend, and daily during July and August.

Weather permitting, you can participate in an eco-activity such as a beach clean-up challenge, a nature discovery activity, eco-crafts for kids, guided nature walks, discover Nurdles, or test your knowledge with the Trivia Wheel. And be sure to check out our beautiful pollinator garden and various displays, too.

We’re looking forward to meeting you at the beach!

Follow us on Facebook, Twitter or Instagram to discover seasonal outreach highlights or give us a call if you have questions: 905 975 5563

Not receiving our monthly e-newsletter yet? Be sure to sign up today to stay connected to local eco-news, events, volunteer opportunities and more!

Thank you to the City of Burlington for supporting our BG Beach Eco-Hub opportunities!
